title: Circle Impossibility FAIL: incorrectly claims a QA state with C=0 exists
_fixture_purpose: Negative fixture: claims (0,5) is a valid QA state with C=0. Validator must catch A1 violation (b=0 not in {1,...,m}) and reject the circle claim.
source_attribution: Intentionally wrong fixture for validation testing.

circle_impossibility.theorem: WRONG: claims C=0 is possible at (0,5)

minimum_C.note: WRONG: b=0 is not a valid QA state

parabola_impossibility.theorem: Included but irrelevant since circle claim is wrong

observer_projection_interpretation.standard_view: Claims circle exists as QA state

witnesses.note: INVALID: b=0 violates A1. Not a valid QA state.

fail_ledger: CI_C_MIN: claims min_C=0 but C=2de>=2 for all valid states
CI_EXHAUSTIVE: all_C_positive is false, contradicts theorem
CI_WITNESS: witness (0,5) has b=0, violating A1 (No-Zero)
